D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

Four Rendering Intents

send the same chroma ramp through each ICC intent and see who clips, who compresses, and who posterises the out-of-gamut end

An ICC intent decides what happens to colours the destination cannot reach. Perceptual compresses the whole range to keep gradients smooth. Relative colorimetric leaves in-gamut colours untouched and clips the rest — risking posterisation. Saturation favours vividness. Absolute also skips white-point adaptation.

Pick the intent and the hue. The top row is the source chroma ramp (out-of-gamut steps marked); the middle row is the mapped output; the curve plots source chroma against output chroma, with the gamut boundary C_b dashed.
